seasonal_notes.April: Very hot and humid, 38-42C. Avoid outdoor activities 10AM-5PM. Stick to museums and air-conditioned venues.
seasonal_notes.May: Extremely hot, 40-45C. Dangerously high temperatures. Avoid outdoor sightseeing entirely during 10AM-5PM. Indoor/AC only.
seasonal_notes.June: Pre-monsoon and monsoon start. Extreme humidity. Midday outdoor activities should be avoided.
seasonal_notes.July: Heavy monsoon. Waterlogging possible. Outdoor parks are muddy and best avoided. Carry rain gear.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon continues. Outdoor walking is difficult. Good for visiting indoor galleries.
seasonal_notes.September: Warm but pleasant. Festive spirit begins. Great for evening walk at Princep Ghat.
seasonal_notes.October: Durga Puja season. Electric atmosphere. Very crowded but pleasant temperature.
seasonal_notes.November: Excellent weather, cool and dry. Ideal for visiting Eco Park and outdoor heritage tours.
seasonal_notes.December: Peak season, very pleasant (15-25C). Christmas carnival at Park Street.
seasonal_notes.January: Cool and comfortable weather. Perfect for walking tours, Princep Ghat, and Eco Park.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild weather, warming up slightly. Good for outdoor sightseeing.
seasonal_notes.March: Warm but tolerable. Morning or late evening outdoor tours recommended.

entry_fees.Victoria Memorial (Palace Museum + Garde: Rs.100 per person (Indians), Free for students in uniform/with school ID
entry_fees.Indian Museum: Rs.70 per person (Indians), Rs.20 for students with ID, camera extra Rs.30
entry_fees.Eco Park: Rs.30 weekdays, Rs.50 weekends & holidays, rides extra
entry_fees.Science City: Rs.60 entry + Rs.150 for Space Theatre
entry_fees.Marble Palace: Free entry (requires 24-hr advance permit from West Bengal Tourism Office)
entry_fees.Princep Ghat: Free entry, country boat ride Rs.200-400 per hour

food.Sharma Dhaba (Ballygunge): Famous for Punjabi meals and tea, Lunch/Dinner (Rs.150-250)
food.Kusum Rolls (Park Street): Famous for egg/chicken kathi rolls, Snack (Rs.60-120)
food.Dilkhusha Cabin (College Street): Kabiraji cutlet and fish fry, Snacks (Rs.100-180)

seasonal_notes.June: Heavy monsoon. Most shacks and water sports closed. Sea is rough and dangerous.
seasonal_notes.July: Peak monsoon. Beautiful green landscape but beaches closed for swimming.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon. Waterfalls at their best. Beaches still not safe for swimming.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. Some shacks reopen. Good for budget travel.
seasonal_notes.October: Post-monsoon. Shacks reopening. Sea calming. Good weather.
seasonal_notes.November: Peak season begins. Perfect weather. All water sports open.
seasonal_notes.December: Most popular month. Christmas and New Year celebrations. Prices spike 30-50%.
seasonal_notes.January: Great weather. Slightly less crowded than December.
seasonal_notes.February: Carnival time in February. Excellent weather.
seasonal_notes.March: Getting warmer. Still good. Last month of peak season.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot and humid, 35-38C. Off season begins. Very cheap rates.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot. Most tourists gone. Cheapest time to visit.

seasonal_notes.April: Very hot, 38-42C. Avoid outdoor monuments 11AM-5PM. Early morning visits only.
seasonal_notes.May: Extremely hot, 42-48C. Avoid most outdoor activities. Stay indoors midday.
seasonal_notes.June: Pre-monsoon heat. 44-46C. Only visit forts at dawn.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon. Cooler but humid. Some forts slippery.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon continues. Scenic green Aravalli hills.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. Still warm but bearable.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather. Navratri and Dussehra celebrations.
seasonal_notes.November: Best time. Cool and clear. Perfect for all sightseeing.
seasonal_notes.December: Peak season. Cool evenings. Slightly crowded.
seasonal_notes.January: Cold nights (8-10C). Warm days. Ideal for forts.
seasonal_notes.February: Holi preparations. Excellent weather.
seasonal_notes.March: Holi festival. Warming up but still good.

food.LMB (Laxmi Misthan Bhandar): Dal baati churma Rs.200-300, sweets Rs.50-150

seasonal_notes.December: Heavy snowfall. Roads to Rohtang and Solang may close. Very cold -5 to 5C. Ideal for snow activities.
seasonal_notes.January: Peak winter. -10 to 2C. Snow everywhere. Limited mobility but beautiful.
seasonal_notes.February: Still very cold. Snow festivals. Great for adventure sports on snow.
seasonal_notes.March: Snow melting. Roads opening. 5-15C. Good time to visit.
seasonal_notes.April: Rohtang Pass opens. Apple blossoms. 10-20C. Excellent.
seasonal_notes.May: Peak season. Rohtang accessible. 15-25C. Very crowded.
seasonal_notes.June: Pre-monsoon. Lush green. 18-28C. Slightly humid.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon. Landslide risk on mountain roads. Avoid Rohtang.
seasonal_notes.August: Peak monsoon. Landslide risk. Some activities suspended.
seasonal_notes.September: Post monsoon. Streams full. Clear skies. 12-22C.
seasonal_notes.October: Autumn colors. Cool weather. 5-18C. Beautiful.
seasonal_notes.November: Early snowfall. Rohtang closing. 0-12C. Quiet and scenic.

entry_fees.Rohtang Pass permit: Rs.500-600 per vehicle (non-Himachali) + Green tax Rs.50

food.Sartaj Fast Food: Rajma chawal Rs.80-120, cheap local food

seasonal_notes.June: Heavy monsoon. Flooding in low-lying areas. Trains may be delayed.
seasonal_notes.July: Peak monsoon. Marine Drive is spectacular in rain but travel is difficult.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon continues. Ganesh Chaturthi celebrations (late August).
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. Ganesh immersion processions. Very festive.
seasonal_notes.October: Post-monsoon. City freshened up. Good weather.
seasonal_notes.November: Best month begins. 25-32C. Perfect for sightseeing.
seasonal_notes.December: Christmas and New Year. Very festive. Slightly crowded.
seasonal_notes.January: Pleasant. 20-30C. Ideal for everything.
seasonal_notes.February: Good weather. 22-32C.
seasonal_notes.March: Holi. Warming up. Still pleasant.
seasonal_notes.April: Getting hot. 30-36C. Humid.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot and humid. 32-38C.

food.Kyani and Co. (Dhobitalao): Bun maska chai Rs.30-60, Irani cafe classics

seasonal_notes.April: Warming up 32-40C. Visit monuments early morning only.
seasonal_notes.May: Extremely hot 42-48C. Loo winds. Avoid all outdoor activities midday.
seasonal_notes.June: Very hot and pre-monsoon dust storms. Only early morning outings.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon begins. Cooler but humid. Flooding in some areas.
seasonal_notes.August: Independence Day celebrations. Monsoon continuing.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. Post-rain Delhi looks great.
seasonal_notes.October: Excellent weather. Navratri and Dussehra festivals.
seasonal_notes.November: Good weather but smog/pollution can be severe in early November.
seasonal_notes.December: Cold 5-20C. Foggy mornings. Carry woolens.
seasonal_notes.January: Coldest month 3-18C. Republic Day parade on Jan 26.
seasonal_notes.February: Warming up. Excellent for sightseeing.
seasonal_notes.March: Holi. Great weather before summer hits.

local_transport.Delhi Metro: Rs.20-60 per trip depending on distance

seasonal_notes.April: Getting hot 35-42C. Dev Deepawali preparations. Early morning ghats are fine.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot 40-46C. Visit ghats only at dawn (5-8AM).
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon begins. Ghats flood slightly. Still atmospheric.
seasonal_notes.July: Heavy monsoon. Ghats partially submerged. Ganga in full flow.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon. Beautiful but difficult travel.
seasonal_notes.September: Post monsoon. Ganga receding. Great atmosphere.
seasonal_notes.October: Navratri and Dussehra. Dev Deepawali in November. Best season.
seasonal_notes.November: Dev Deepawali - most spectacular time. Ghats lit with 1 million lamps.
seasonal_notes.December: Cool 8-22C. Pleasant for ghat walks.
seasonal_notes.January: Cold 5-18C. Morning aarti is magical in winter fog.
seasonal_notes.February: Maha Shivratri celebrated with great fervor.
seasonal_notes.March: Holi in Vrindavan nearby. Good weather.

seasonal_notes.January: Very cold 2-8C. Snow possible on higher peaks. Clear Kanchenjunga views.
seasonal_notes.February: Cold but clear. Cherry blossoms begin.
seasonal_notes.March: Spring. Rhododendrons blooming. 8-16C. Excellent.
seasonal_notes.April: Best weather. 12-20C. Clear mountain views. Tea gardens lush.
seasonal_notes.May: Pre-monsoon. Slightly hazy but warm. 14-22C.
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon begins. Leeches on trails. Avoid trekking.
seasonal_notes.July: Heavy rain and fog. Landslide risk on mountain roads.
seasonal_notes.August: Peak monsoon. Very limited visibility. Not recommended.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. Trails muddy. Starting to clear.
seasonal_notes.October: Post-monsoon clarity. Best Kanchenjunga views. 10-18C.
seasonal_notes.November: Clear skies. Cool. 6-14C. Second best season.
seasonal_notes.December: Cold 2-10C. Possible snow. Very peaceful.

seasonal_notes.January: Cold 4-15C. River rafting suspended. Peaceful for yoga.
seasonal_notes.February: Maha Shivratri festival. 8-20C. Rafting resumes.
seasonal_notes.March: Excellent. 15-28C. River high enough for rafting.
seasonal_notes.April: International Yoga Festival in March/April. Best for adventure.
seasonal_notes.May: Hot 28-36C but river sports still good. Pre-monsoon.
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon beginning. Char Dham Yatra season. Crowded.
seasonal_notes.July: Heavy monsoon. River floods. All rafting suspended. Avoid.
seasonal_notes.August: Peak monsoon. Flash floods possible. Avoid adventure activities.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon ending. River receding. Rafting may resume late September.
seasonal_notes.October: Post-monsoon. Best for rafting and trekking. 15-28C.
seasonal_notes.November: Good weather. 10-22C. Rivers calming. Good yoga retreat.
seasonal_notes.December: Cold 4-18C. Very peaceful. Yoga centers active.

seasonal_notes.April: Baisakhi festival in April. Warm 28-36C.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot 38-45C. Avoid outdoor activities midday.
seasonal_notes.June: Extremely hot 40-46C. Only visit Golden Temple early morning.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon. Slight relief from heat. 30-36C.
seasonal_notes.August: Independence Day. Wagah border ceremony very special.
seasonal_notes.September: Post-monsoon. Cooling down. 28-36C.
seasonal_notes.October: Excellent. 18-28C. Diwali season.
seasonal_notes.November: Great weather. 10-22C.
seasonal_notes.December: Cold 4-16C. Foggy mornings. Very atmospheric at Golden Temple.
seasonal_notes.January: Coldest month. 2-14C. Republic Day Wagah parade very special.
seasonal_notes.February: Good weather. 10-22C.
seasonal_notes.March: Holi. Warming up. Last good month.

seasonal_notes.January: Pleasant weather, ideal for sightseeing and outdoor activities like boat rides on Lake Pichola.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild winters, perfect for exploring the city on foot or by bicycle.
seasonal_notes.March: Temperature starts rising, but still a good time to visit before the summer heat sets in.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot days and warm nights, try to avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, avoid visiting during this month if possible.
seasonal_notes.June: Summer is at its peak, monsoon is expected to start by the end of the month.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season, expect heavy rainfall and potential disruptions in travel.
seasonal_notes.August: Continued monsoon, scenic landscapes but potential for flooding.
seasonal_notes.September: Weather starts to cool down, a good time to visit before the tourist season starts.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather returns, ideal for all outdoor activities.
seasonal_notes.November: Cool and pleasant, perfect for sightseeing and exploring the city.
seasonal_notes.December: Mild winters, a great time to enjoy the city's festive atmosphere.

entry_fees.City Palace: Rs. 30 per person (Indians), Rs. 15 for students with ID
entry_fees.Lake Pichola: Free, but boat ride costs Rs. 400-600 per person

seasonal_notes.January: Cool and pleasant, ideal for sightseeing. Average temperature ranges from 2-15°C.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild winter, good for outdoor activities. Average temperature ranges from 10-20°C.
seasonal_notes.March: Starting to get warm, but still pleasant. Average temperature ranges from 15-25°C.
seasonal_notes.April: Getting hotter, but still manageable. Average temperature ranges from 20-30°C.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, temperatures often reach 45°C. Avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours.
seasonal_notes.June: Extremely hot, temperatures can reach 48°C. Stay hydrated and avoid outdoor activities.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season starts, temperatures decrease, but humidity increases. Average temperature ranges from 25-35°C.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon season continues, temperatures and humidity remain high. Average temperature ranges from 25-35°C.
seasonal_notes.September: Monsoon season ends, temperatures start to decrease. Average temperature ranges from 20-30°C.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather, ideal for sightseeing. Average temperature ranges from 15-25°C.
seasonal_notes.November: Cool and pleasant, ideal for outdoor activities. Average temperature ranges from 10-20°C.
seasonal_notes.December: Cool winter, good for sightseeing. Average temperature ranges from 5-15°C.

entry_fees.Agra Fort: Rs. 50 per person (Indians), Rs. 20 for students with ID

seasonal_notes.January: Pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor activities like visiting Lalbagh Botanical Garden or taking a stroll at Cubbon Park.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild climate, perfect for exploring the city's cultural attractions like the Bangalore Palace and ISKCON Temple.
seasonal_notes.March: Temperature starts rising, but still a good time to visit famous landmarks like Tipu Sultan's Summer Palace.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot and humid, avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours, prefer visiting air-conditioned malls like Orion Mall or Phoenix MarketCity.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, try to stay indoors during the day, visit places like Visvesvaraya Industrial and Technological Museum in the evening.
seasonal_notes.June: Start of monsoon season, expect occasional rain showers, visit indoor attractions like the National Gallery of Modern Art.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season, heavy rainfall expected, prefer indoor activities like visiting the Karnataka Chitrakala Parishath art gallery.
seasonal_notes.August: Continuation of monsoon season, flooded roads possible, avoid traveling during peak rain hours.
seasonal_notes.September: End of monsoon season, pleasant weather returns, ideal for outdoor activities like visiting the Bannerghatta Biological Park.
seasonal_notes.October: Festive season begins, pleasant weather, ideal time to visit famous landmarks and attend cultural events.
seasonal_notes.November: Cool and pleasant climate, perfect for exploring the city's many lakes and gardens.
seasonal_notes.December: Mild winter, ideal for outdoor activities and visiting popular tourist spots like the Bangalore Fort.

entry_fees.Lalbagh Botanical Garden: Rs. 20 per person (Indians), Rs. 10 for students with ID
entry_fees.Bangalore Palace: Rs. 230 per person (Indians), Rs. 115 for students with ID

seasonal_notes.November: Cool and pleasant, ideal for outdoor activities. Average temperature: 12-25°C.

seasonal_notes.March: Temperature starts rising, but still comfortable for outdoor activities. Average temperature ranges from 20-30°C.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot weather starts setting in, but still manageable for sightseeing. Average temperature ranges from 25-35°C.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, temperatures often reach 40°C. Avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours.
seasonal_notes.June: Extremely hot, temperatures can reach up to 45°C. Not recommended for travel.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season starts, but Jaisalmer receives very little rainfall. Temperatures are still high, but humidity increases.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon season continues, but rainfall is scarce. Temperatures start decreasing slightly.
seasonal_notes.September: Weather starts cooling down, making it comfortable for outdoor activities again. Average temperature ranges from 20-30°C.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather, ideal for sightseeing and outdoor activities. Average temperature ranges from 15-25°C.

seasonal_notes.January: Pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor activities like visiting Mehrangarh Fort and exploring the city. Average temperature ranges from 10-24°C.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild and sunny, perfect for sightseeing. Temperature ranges from 12-27°C.
seasonal_notes.March: Gradually warming up, with temperatures between 18-32°C. Still a good time to visit before the summer heat sets in.
seasonal_notes.April: Getting hotter, with temperatures often reaching 35-40°C. It's advisable to stay hydrated and plan indoor activities during the peak sun hours.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, with temperatures often above 42°C. It's best to avoid outdoor activities during the day and opt for early morning or evening explorations.
seasonal_notes.June: The start of the monsoon season, with temperatures still high but humidity increasing. It might not be the best time for outdoor activities due to the heat and potential rains.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season, with frequent rains and high humidity. While it can be a good time for those who enjoy the rains, outdoor activities might be limited.
seasonal_notes.August: Continuation of the monsoon season, with temperatures slightly decreasing but still quite humid. It's a good time for indoor activities and visiting museums or historical sites.
seasonal_notes.September: The monsoon starts to recede, with temperatures becoming more pleasant, ranging from 22-32°C. It's a good time to visit before the peak tourist season starts.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather, with a range of 15-30°C, making it ideal for outdoor activities and sightseeing.
seasonal_notes.November: Cool and sunny, with temperatures between 12-28°C, perfect for exploring the city and its landmarks.
seasonal_notes.December: Mild winter, with temperatures ranging from 10-25°C, making it a great time to visit Jodhpur.

seasonal_notes.October: Cool and pleasant weather, festive season, ideal for sightseeing and exploring the city.
seasonal_notes.November: Mild winter, perfect for trekking, sightseeing, and outdoor activities.
seasonal_notes.December: Cool and pleasant weather, ideal for trekking, sightseeing, and relaxation.

entry_fees.Mullayanagiri: Rs. 50 per person (Indians), Rs. 20 for students with ID

seasonal_notes.January: Cold weather, ideal for snow lovers, avoid trekking and outdoor activities due to freezing temperatures
seasonal_notes.February: Still cold, but slightly warmer, good time for visiting monasteries and indoor attractions
seasonal_notes.March: Pleasant weather, ideal for trekking and outdoor activities, blooming flowers and greenery
seasonal_notes.April: Mild and sunny, perfect for sightseeing and adventure sports
seasonal_notes.May: Warm and sunny, ideal for trekking and outdoor activities, but can be crowded
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon season, heavy rainfall, landslides, and roadblocks, avoid traveling
seasonal_notes.July: Continuous rainfall, high humidity, and cloudy weather, not ideal for sightseeing
seasonal_notes.August: Still rainy, but slightly better, can be a good time for budget travelers and nature lovers
seasonal_notes.September: Pleasant weather, clear skies, and mild temperatures, ideal for trekking and outdoor activities
seasonal_notes.October: Cool and sunny, perfect for sightseeing and adventure sports, festive atmosphere
seasonal_notes.November: Crisp and cold, ideal for snow lovers, good time for visiting monasteries and indoor attractions
seasonal_notes.December: Very cold, freezing temperatures, avoid trekking and outdoor activities

entry_fees.Namgyal Institute of Tibetology: Rs. 10 per person (Indians), free for students with ID

seasonal_notes.January: Cool and dry, ideal for trekking and outdoor activities. Average temperature ranges from 6-18°C.
seasonal_notes.February: Pleasant weather, good for sightseeing and visiting local markets. Temperature ranges from 8-20°C.
seasonal_notes.March: Mild and sunny, perfect for exploring the city and its surroundings. Temperature ranges from 12-24°C.
seasonal_notes.April: Warm and sunny, great for visiting waterfalls and lakes. Temperature ranges from 15-28°C.
seasonal_notes.May: Hot and humid, avoid outdoor activities during peak sun hours. Temperature ranges from 18-30°C.
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon season begins, heavy rainfall and landslides are common. Temperature ranges from 15-25°C.
seasonal_notes.July: Continuous rainfall, difficult to travel and explore the city. Temperature ranges from 12-22°C.
seasonal_notes.August: Rainfall continues, but the scenery is lush and green. Temperature ranges from 10-20°C.
seasonal_notes.September: Weather starts to clear up, pleasant and cool. Temperature ranges from 10-22°C.
seasonal_notes.October: Ideal weather, cool and dry, perfect for trekking and outdoor activities. Temperature ranges from 8-20°C.
seasonal_notes.November: Pleasant and sunny, great for sightseeing and visiting local markets. Temperature ranges from 10-22°C.
seasonal_notes.December: Cool and dry, good for exploring the city and its surroundings. Temperature ranges from 6-18°C.

seasonal_notes.January: Cool and pleasant weather, ideal for visiting temples and beaches. Avoid water activities during high tide.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild winter, perfect for outdoor activities like sunbathing and beach walks.
seasonal_notes.March: Temperature starts rising, but still a good time to visit. Avoid visiting during Holi festival for crowded streets.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot and humid, but the city is less crowded. Prefer indoor activities during the day.
seasonal_notes.May: Peak summer, extremely hot and humid. Avoid visiting during this time if possible.
seasonal_notes.June: Monsoon starts, heavy rainfall expected. Prefer indoor activities and avoid water activities.
seasonal_notes.July: Continuous rainfall, but the city is lush green. Avoid visiting during peak monsoon.
seasonal_notes.August: Rainfall decreases, but still humid. Prefer indoor activities during the day.
seasonal_notes.September: Weather starts to cool down, ideal for visiting temples and beaches.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather, perfect for outdoor activities like beach walks and sunbathing.
seasonal_notes.November: Cool and pleasant weather, ideal for visiting temples and beaches. Avoid water activities during high tide.
seasonal_notes.December: Mild winter, perfect for outdoor activities like sunbathing and beach walks.

seasonal_notes.January: Pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor activities like visiting the Sabarmati Ashram and exploring the old city. Average temperature ranges from 12-25 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.February: Mild winter, perfect for visiting the Ahmedabad Flower Show and exploring the city's gardens. Average temperature ranges from 15-28 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.March: Temperature starts rising, but still a good time to visit the city's museums and indoor attractions. Average temperature ranges from 20-32 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.April: Hot and dry, avoid outdoor activities during the peak sun hours. Average temperature ranges from 25-38 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.May: Very hot, prefer indoor activities and stay hydrated. Average temperature ranges from 28-42 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.June: Start of the monsoon season, expect occasional rain showers. Average temperature ranges from 28-38 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.July: Monsoon season, some outdoor activities might be restricted due to rain. Average temperature ranges from 25-32 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.August: Monsoon season, expect frequent rain showers. Average temperature ranges from 24-30 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.September: End of the monsoon season, pleasant weather returns. Average temperature ranges from 22-30 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.October: Pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor activities like visiting the Navratri celebrations. Average temperature ranges from 20-30 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.November: Mild winter, perfect for visiting the city's historical landmarks. Average temperature ranges from 18-28 degrees Celsius.
seasonal_notes.December: Cool winter, ideal for exploring the city's markets and trying street food. Average temperature ranges from 12-25 degrees Celsius.
